data.title: T-Max Lending Saves Sacramento Project with $18 Million Loan, Proving Unrivaled Financing Prowess
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — T-Max Lending, a premier direct private lender in San Diego, California, has closed an $18 million loan for a 150-unit multi-family development in Elk Grove, Sacramento County, rescuing it from foreclosure. This high-stakes deal showcases T-Max Lending’s ability to deliver fast, reliable financing where others falter, cementing its status as California’s standout lender.

This project’s development began with the property purchased in July 2021. The plans for the project received full approval in December 2022. However, litigation delays pushed the project back an additional 18 months, causing considerable financial strain on the borrower. The borrower faced foreclosure after three other lenders issued commitment letters they couldn’t fulfill. His current lender, fed up with broken promises, offered no more leeway. He was referred to T-Max Lending by a client who closed a $15.5 million hotel development with T-Max in Charleston, SC, in February 2025. That Charleston referral, praising T-Max’s flawless, rapid execution, erased the borrower’s doubts. “After hearing how smooth that $15.5 million deal went, I knew T-Max was my shot,” he said. T-Max proved it, closing the loan at breakneck speed despite the tight timeline.

Needing $18 million, he sought $15.8 million to clear his lender (with accrued interest) and $2 million for site prep: undergrounding power poles, code updates, demolition, abatement, and grading. T-Max Lending acted fast, delivering a 24-month, 9.5% interest-only loan with an interest reserve, ensuring the developer could go vertical without cash-flow stress.

“We understand that timing is everything, especially in real estate development,” said Kenneth Richards, CEO of T-Max Lending. “We saw a client who’d poured everything into this project, and we weren’t about to let it slip away. That’s what sets us apart, we deliver. This is a perfect example of how T-Max Lending works tirelessly to provide solutions where others can’t or won’t.”

The Sacramento development is poised to make a significant impact on the local community by adding much-needed rental housing units to the area. Located in a high-demand residential market, the 180 unit project is expected to meet the growing need for affordable and well-constructed housing in Sacramento, contributing to the city's economic development and growth. The project is strategically located near essential services, transit access, and key employment hubs, making it a prime location for prospective residents.

T-Max Lending’s proactive approach to financing and its focus on understanding the unique challenges faced by borrowers set it apart from other lenders. As a direct private lender, T-Max Lending remains committed to providing fast, flexible, and reliable financial solutions, especially when dealing with high-pressure situations like the one faced by this Sacramento developer. By expediting funding and offering a customized loan solution, T-Max Lending demonstrated its ability to navigate complex challenges and deliver results.

This deal underscores T-Max Lending’s reputation as a leader in the private lending sector, particularly in California. The company continues to expand its footprint by providing tailored solutions to developers, investors, and borrowers, helping to drive forward-thinking projects and foster growth in the state’s ever-evolving real estate market.

For more information about T-Max Lending and its loan products, visit www.tmaxlending.com

About T-Max Lending

T-Max Lending is a direct private lender based in San Diego, CA, offering flexible financing solutions for residential, commercial, and mixed-use real estate projects. With a focus on quick and efficient loan processing, T-Max Lending provides funding for a variety of real estate needs, including development, construction, and refinancing. The company prides itself on its ability to deliver financing solutions where traditional lenders may hesitate, ensuring that its clients have the capital they need to succeed. From Charleston’s $15.5 million hotel to Sacramento’s $18 million lifeline, T-Max turns challenges into wins with speed and precision.

For media inquiries, please contact:

T-Max Lending LLC

619 259 0177

Info@tmaxlending.com

www.tmaxlending.com

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/bbc9f595-0a3f-44f4-9f3f-23fd06fbc629

Closed Deal

T-Max Lending LLC Closed deal

data.title: Takashimaya Profit Rises 25% in Fiscal 2025
data.description:
Takashimaya Co. reported a 25% rise in profit attributable to owners of the parent to 39.5 billion yen for the fiscal year ended Feb. 28, from 31.6 billion yen a year ago, driven by strong sales growth and cost efficiencies.

The department store operator's earnings per share (EPS) rose to 107.25 yen from 85.27 yen a year ago, according to a Monday bourse filing.

Operating revenue increased 6.9% to 498.5 billion yen from 466.1 billion yen a year earlier, supported by robust domestic demand and inbound tourism. Total operating revenue gained 8.5% to 1.032 trillion yen.

For the fiscal year ending Feb. 28, 2026, the company expects attributable profit to grow 1.2% to 40.0 billion yen or 131.85 yen per share, while operating revenue is projected to rise 4.6% to 521.2 billion yen. Total operating revenue is expected to gain 3.6% to 1.07 trillion yen.

Takashimaya plans to pay interim and year-end dividends of 13 yen per share, each, for the current fiscal year.

It declared a final dividend of 13 yen per share, payable from May 21, after adjusting for a 2-for-1 stock split in September 2024.

data.title: Kazatomprom to Supply Natural Uranium to Czech Energy Company ČEZ
data.description:
Uranium miner Kazatomprom signed a deal to supply natural uranium to Czech energy company ČEZ (CEZ.PR, ), according to a Tuesday filing.

The supply from Kazakhstan will meet one-third of the uranium needed for the fuel assemblies, developed by Westinghouse, for the Temelín nuclear power plant in the Czech Republic over the next seven years.

data.title: Ageas funds esure acquisition with 550 million euro share sale
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Belgian insurer Ageas has raised 550 million euros ($624 million) to finance its acquisition of esure via an accelerated bookbuild on Monday evening.

Launched after the market close in Brussels on Monday evening, the share sale consisted of 10.96 million new shares, equivalent to a 5.8% capital increase for Ageas.

The offer price was 50.15 euros, a discount of 5.4% to the closing price of Ageas on Monday evening. The size of the deal was increased from an initial target of 525 million euros due to the strength of the demand.

Bank of America and Deutsche Bank were bookrunners.

Ageas’ capital raising is the first major share sale in Europe since U.S. President Donald Trump imposed sweeping trade tariffs almost a fortnight ago, triggering a global equity market selloff.

The proceeds of the share sale will be used to partly finance Ageas’ acquisition of UK P&C insurer esure, which it announced on Monday.

Ageas has agreed to pay Bain Capital 1.3 billion pounds ($1.7 billion) for esure, which it said it would finance through a mixture of spare cash and new hybrid debt or equity.

The Belgian insurer has also been provided with a two-year bridge loan facility by Deutsche Bank and Bank of America.

The deal is the latest attempt by Ageas to scale up in the UK personal lines market following two rebuffed bids for Direct Line last year.

In an interview with The Insurer on Monday, Ageas UK CEO Ant Middle said the deal for esure was the “right opportunity at the right time" for the Belgian insurer.

The deal is expected to close in the second half of 2025, subject to regulatory approvals.

Shares in Ageas were trading at 50.80 euros as of 9:14 a.m. (0814 GMT) in Brussels on Tuesday, down 4.1% from the previous close, giving Ageas a market capitalisation of 9.54 billion euros.

data.title: Barclays double-upgrades 'tariff-proof' NKT
data.description:
** Barclays double upgrades its rating on Danish power cable solutions NKT to "buy" from "sell"

** Broker points to NKT's "discounted" valuation and says that the company, in its view, will become tariff-proof from 2026

** Stock up around 5%, among best performers on STOXX 600 index

** Amid looming economic uncertainties, NKT stands out with leading non-cyclical exposure and 83%/86% of group sales/EBITDA coming from transmission and distribution, it says

** Barclays highlights NKT's sector-leading exposure to Germany and sees total tariff cost at 8 mln euro

** It notes that elevated capex may keep near-term cash performance muted but supports return on invested capital in the longer run

** Broker raises its PT for NKT's stock to DKK 597 per share, 23% above Monday's close price

data.title: Gocom Information Technology Consortium Wins Mine Automation Bid
data.description:
Hefei Gocom Information Technology said its consortium won a 77.0-million-yuan automation contract for the Shaling gold mine in China's Shandong province.

The company will provide intelligent mining services as part of the joint bid with Changchun Gold Design Institute, according to a Tuesday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the company closed 1% higher Tuesday.

data.title: European Midday Briefing: Stocks Edge Higher on Tariff-Exemption Hopes
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Stocks:

Stocks in Europe climbed amid tentative hopes for leniency in President Trump's tariff agenda.

Trump on Monday said he is considering some short-term exemptions to his 25% tariff on foreign-made vehicle imports, following exemptions for smartphones and other electronics over the weekend.

U.K. unemployment data showed wage growth picking up in the first few months of the year, suggesting that continued high inflation will remain a concern for the Bank of England.

Other data releases to watch on Tuesday include European industrial production figures and German ZEW survey of economic sentiment.

Economic Insight

The escalating trade war and strained U.S.-China relations could spur a sharper global slowdown , S&P Global said.

UBS downgraded China's 2025 economic growth forecast to 3.4%, from 4%, assuming current tariff hikes remain and China rolls out additional stimulus.

The bank anticipates China's exports to the U.S. to fall by two thirds in coming quarters and overall exports to fall 10%.

Market Insight

Shares in luxury stocks fell after industry bellwether LVMH on Monday reported sales that missed analysts expectations, fueling concerns about U.S. demand.

European car stocks climbed on Trump's suggestion of an exemtpion to auto tariffs.

U.S. Markets:

Stock futures edged higher amid optimism about Trump's tariff exemptions.

Bank of America, Citigroup and Johnson & Johnson will all report 1Q earnings.

Forex:

The euro slipped against the dollar.

The currency looks overbought and overvalued, ING sad, but added that it still has scope to extend its recent gains.

High liquidity should allow it to continue absorbing the recent safe-haven flight from the dollar.

The dollar recovered slightly on hopes for more lenient tariffs.

Pepperstone said to continue selling the currency on any rallies, as it still isn't acting like a safe haven and the idea of U.S. exceptionalism is "now stone dead."

The bulk of dollar selling in recent days has been confined within the London and Tokyo trading sessions, suggesting international investors are seeking an exit from the U.S., it added.

Sterling rose against the euro.

Bonds:

German Bund yields fell.

Eurozone government bonds, especially Bunds, should benefit from the tail risk scenario of shifting demand away from Treasurys, JPMorgan said.

"We can easily see 10-year German yield getting close to or even below 2.0% under a global recession scenario, but wait for some clarity before making forecast changes."

Commerzbank said Bunds seem keen to move back into the range occupied before Germany's announcement of high-scale fiscal plans.

Domestic drivers for German government bonds on Tuesday include the ZEW economic sentiment indicators for April, as well as the auction of April 2030-dated Bobl with 4.5 billion euros on offer.

Yields on U.K. government bonds fell, following similar moves in the Treasurys and eurozone government bonds.

Bond markets are calming after last week's volatility and in anticipation of more interest-rate cuts due to a slowing economy.

Energy:

Oil prices rose, contining to recover from multi-year lows as investors parse Tariff chaos.

Traders were also keeping an eye on Iranian oil exports, after Treasury officials told Singapore's banks and shippers to expect increased pressure on Iran's oil industry, ANZ said.

Metals:

Gold futures rose close to record highs on persistent safe-haven demand amid trade tensions.

The Chinese central bank reportedly issued new gold import quotas to commercial banks.

A rally in comex gold futures is likely to stay intact, RHB Retail Research said, noting sustained buying interest.

The commodity is trading comfortably above the 20-day and 50-day simple moving averages. Barring a break below support at $3,000/oz, the precious metal is expected to rise toward $3,300/oz in the near term, RHB added.

EMEA HEADLINES

Luxury Empire LVMH Posts Revenue Below Views Amid Global Trade War Threat

Louis Vuitton owner LVMH posted sales below analysts' forecasts for the first quarter as a slowdown that has pervaded the luxury-goods industry for months lingers on.

The conglomerate owned by French billionaire Bernard Arnault reported revenue of 20.31 billion euros ($23.08 billion) for the first three months of the year, down 3% organically compared to the prior-year period.

TotalEnergies Expects First-Quarter Production Increase, Uptick in Refining Margin

TotalEnergies expects first-quarter hydrocarbon production to be at the high end of its quarterly guidance range and for earnings to be boosted by an on-quarter increase in refining margins.

The French energy major said Tuesday that it expects hydrocarbon production for the first three months of 2025 to be at the high end of its 2.5 million to 2.55 million barrels of oil-equivalent a day guidance range. This would be an increase on the 2.43 million oil-equivalent barrels a day it produced in the fourth quarter of last year, when it benefited from the ramp up of projects that had started earlier in the year.

Beiersdorf Sticks to Outlook After Solid Sales Growth

Beiersdorf reiterated its full-year expectations after reporting an increase in first-quarter sales, mainly driven by its skin and body-care segment.

The German personal-care products maker on Tuesday said it continues to expect organic sales growth in 2025 of 4% to 6%.

Ericsson Posts Rise in North American Network Spending Ahead of Tariffs

STOCKHOLM-Ericsson's first-quarter sales were boosted by some North American customers rushing to buy network equipment as they moved forward investments ahead of planned U.S. import tariffs.

The Swedish telecommunications-equipment company said Tuesday that its key network business posted a 6% rise in network sales overall in the period, with strong growth in North America offsetting significant declines in India as well as weakness in the Middle East and Africa.

Publicis Confirms Outlook After Organic Growth Beats Expectations

Publicis confirmed its outlook for the year after first-quarter organic growth beat expectations, amid fears that tariff uncertainty might prompt clients to pull back spending.

The French advertising and marketing-services group said Tuesday that net revenue grew 4.9% organically for the first quarter, against analysts' expectations of a 4.5% rise, according to consensus estimates compiled by the company. A solid performance in the U.S., with organic growth at 4.1%, contributed to the result.

GLOBAL NEWS

Global Markets Stabilize; Carmakers Rise on Tariff Reprieve Hopes

Markets were relatively stable Tuesday compared with last week's extreme volatility, with gains across most major Asian bourses and a modestly higher open in Europe.

U.S. stock futures also nudged higher, while the dollar recovered slightly and the 10-year Treasury yield eased.

IEA Cuts Oil Demand Forecast as U.S. Tariff Weigh on Economic Growth

Oil demand growth is forecast to be weaker than previously projected as escalating global trade tensions sparked by U.S. President Trump's tariffs weigh on the global economy, the International Energy Agency said.

"With arduous trade negotiations expected to take place during the coming 90-day reprieve on tariffs and possibly beyond, oil markets are in for a bumpy ride and considerable uncertainties hang over our forecasts for this year and next," according to the Paris-based agency.

U.S. Strikes Spur Plans for Yemeni Ground War Against Houthis

Yemeni militias are planning a ground offensive against the Houthis in an attempt to take advantage of a U.S. bombing campaign that has degraded the militant group's capabilities, Yemeni and U.S. officials say.

The Yemeni factions are sensing an opportunity to oust the Houthis from at least parts of the Red Sea coast they have controlled in the decade since they took power over much of the country's northwest, the officials said.

Israel Has Taken Over About a Third of the Gaza Strip

Israeli troops have taken over about a third of the Gaza Strip in renewed military action, declaring security zones in swaths of the north and south while pushing out their populations as part of a new strategy to ratchet up pressure on Hamas.

After relying mainly on airstrikes and tactical raids for the first year and a half of the war, Israel is now seizing land and threatening to hold it indefinitely as it presses Hamas to release the hostages still held in Gaza.

Write to don.forbes@wsj.com

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Wishbone Gold Completes Subsidiary Reorganisation in Australia
data.description:
Wishbone Gold completed the reorganization of its Wishbone Gold WA subsidiary in Australia after settling all legal liabilities, according to a Tuesday filing.

As part of the reorganization, the London-listed mining company has taken back full ownership and operational control of the Red Setter and Cottesloe projects.

data.title: Sweden's Yubico Appoints New CFO
data.description:
Swedish authentication technology company Yubico appointed Snejana Koleva as its new chief financial officer, succeeding Camilla Öberg.

Koleva, who is currently the CFO of Transcom, will take up the role no later than Oct. 13, according to a Tuesday release.

data.title: India's NCL Industries up on strong Q4 business update
data.description:
** Shares of cement maker NCL Industries rise as much as 6% to 204.78 rupees, highest since February 11

** Co on Friday said its Q4 cement production was up 8% Y/Y and sales rose 7% Y/Y

** Stock above 50-day and 100-day exponential moving averages

** Stock last up 5.1%, cutting YTD loss to 7.4%

data.title: TotalEnergies Secures 15-year Deal to Supply LNG in Dominican Republic
data.description:
TotalEnergies signed an agreement with utility Energia Natural Dominicana to deliver 400,000 tons of liquefied natural gas per year in the Dominican Republic.

The French oil and gas company will supply LNG to the joint venture between AES Dominicana and Energa for 15 years from mid-2027, with the price indexed to Henry Hub, according to a Tuesday release.

The natural gas will power a 470-megawatt combined-cycle power plant currently under construction in the Caribbean country.

data.title: Beiersdorf's Consumer Business Shows ResilienceMarket Talk
data.description: Beiersdorf's 1Q performance was better than some had feared, with its core consumer segment sales growth in line with recently downgraded expectations, Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. The German personal-care products maker's revenue was 1.2% ahead of market expectations, with organic sales growth of 3.6% also beating views. Consumer organic sales growth was 2.3% against a consensus of 2.4%, they say. "We expect a small relief rally in the stock, but still see downside risk to consensus and the shares," they say. Shares are up 0.9%, but down more than 10% over the last 52 weeks. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

data.title: REG - Genuit Group PLC - Notice of AGM
data.description:
RNS Number : 0918F Genuit Group PLC 15 April 2025  

15 April 2025

Genuit Group plc

Publication of 2024 Annual Report and Accounts  

and Notice of 2025 Annual General Meeting

 

 

Genuit Group plc (the "Company") announces that its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2024 and Notice of its 2025 Annual General Meeting ("AGM") have today been submitted to the National Storage Mechanism, where they will shortly be available for inspection at https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism.  

Copies of these documents are also available to view on the Company's website at www.genuitgroup.com.

Copies of these documents are being posted or made available to the Company's shareholders today.

The Company currently plans to hold its AGM at 1.00pm on Monday 19 May 2025 at its offices in Leeds at 4 Victoria Place, Holbeck, Leeds, LS11 5AE, United Kingdom. Further details can be found in the formal Notice of Meeting.  

Enquiries:                        

Emma Versluys,

Group Legal Counsel and Company Secretary                        +44 (0) 1138 315315                                                              

Notes to Editors:

Genuit Group plc ("Genuit", the "Company" or the "Group"), is the UK's largest provider of sustainable water, climate and ventilation products for the built environment. Genuit's solutions allow customers to mitigate and adapt to the effects of climate change and meet evolving sustainability regulations and targets.

The Group is divided into three Business Units, each of which addresses specific challenges in the built environment:

·   Sustainable Building Solutions - Providing a range of construction solutions to reduce the carbon content of the built environment.

·   Water Management Solutions - Driving climate adaptation and resilience through integrated surface and drainage solutions.

·   Climate Management Solutions - Addressing the drivers for low carbon heating and cooling, and clean and healthy air ventilation.

Across these Business Units, Genuit's brands are some of the most well-established and innovative in the industry, including Polypipe, Nuaire and Adey.

The Group primarily serves the UK and European building and construction markets, with a presence in Italy and the Netherlands, and sells to specific niches in the rest of the world.

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

data.title: North Macedonia's Oilko KDA plans flat dividend for 2024
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), April 15 (SeeNews) - North Macedonia's Oilko KDA Skopje [MSE:OILK], which is active in transport and distribution of oil products, said on Tuesday it plans to distribute a gross dividend of 600 denars ($11.1/9.8 euro) per share for 2024, same as the previous year.

Under the proposal, the company will distribute a total of 141.5 million denars as dividend for last year, Oilko said in a bourse filing.

The company's shareholders will vote on the proposal at a general meeting scheduled for May 21.

Oilko's net profit net profit fell 15% on the year to 202.8 million denars in 2024.

The company's shares have not traded on the Macedonian Stock Exchange (MSE) in the past year.

(1 euro = 61.51 denars)

data.title: Singapore Trims 2025 GDP Outlook Amid Global Tariff Wars
data.description:
Singapore downgraded its growth forecast for the year amid risks brought by expanded tariffs from the US.

The Ministry and Trade and Industry brought down its 2025 gross domestic product or GDP outlook to between 0% to 2%, a reduction from the previously predicted GDP growth of between 1% and 3% a month ago, according to a Monday press release on the Department of Statistics' webpage.

Singapore believes that economies within the Asia Pacific region will be negatively affected by weak demand brought about by the tariffs. US President Donald Trump is now considering including smartphones, semiconductors, and other electronics in the tariffs, which were hiked to 125% on goods coming from China.

For the first quarter, the growth of Singapore's GDP slowed by 3.8% year on year, lower than the 5% growth in the previous quarter.

The manufacturing sector climbed 5% year on year, a moderation from the 7.4% expansion in the previous quarter, while the construction sector rose 4.6%, higher than the 4.4% growth in the previous quarter.

Meanwhile, almost all of the services sectors expanded during the same period, except for retail trade.

data.title: Ferrotec Holdings Corp: To Invest $226.4 Million To Build New Chip Equipment Related Product Plant In Malaysia, Start Operations In Sept 2026
data.description:
Ferrotec Holdings Corp :

  • FERROTEC HOLDINGS CORP - TO INVEST $226.4 MILLION TO BUILD NEW CHIP EQUIPMENT RELATED PRODUCT PLANT IN MALAYSIA, START OPERATIONS IN SEPT 2026

data.title: Quality Power Bags New Order Worth INR197 Million; Shares Jump 7%
data.description:
High-voltage electrical equipment manufacturer Quality Power Electrical Equipments has secured an order worth 197 million Indian rupees from an undisclosed customer, according to a Tuesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The contract is for the supply of reactors for power transmission - coil products for flexible AC transmission systems application. The delivery of the project is expected to be completed in 15-18 months.

The company's shares were up nearly 7% in recent trade.

data.title: Redeye: CoinShares Q1 2025 Preview - Oversold on price action
data.description:
Redeye has revised its estimates for CoinShares in anticipation of its Q1 2025 report, scheduled for 13 May 2025. Due to adverse price movements during the quarter, Redeye has lowered its assumptions for asset management. Minor adjustments have also been made to the CSCM estimates. Consequently, EPS and EBIT projections for 2025e–2028e have been reduced by 11–14%. CoinShares is currently trading at an EV/EBITDA multiple of 4.1x, P/E ratio of 5.4x , and offers a dividend yield of 5.8% on 2026e. CoinShares is only indirectly affected by tariffs and with an NCAV projected at SEK64 for 2025e versus share price ~SEK71, Redeye views the risk/reward positively skewed and that the share price has more than compensated for lower asset management fees.

Read more and download the Research Update.

Follow companies at Redeye to receive the latest equity research within Life Science and Technology.

This is a press release from Redeye - Research Powered Investment Banking. www.redeye.se/
